images.subject: An extreme close-up of a single human eye, looking directly forward. The image captures the pupil, iris, sclera, upper and lower eyelids with eyelashes, and a thick eyebrow. The eye is wide open.
images.composition: The photograph is a tightly framed, extreme close-up, centered on the spread. It is bisected vertically by the book's gutter, splitting the pupil and iris. The image is rendered in high-contrast, grainy black and white, emphasizing the textures of skin and hair.

design.layout_description: A single, massive photograph of an eye dominates the spread, spanning both pages and split by the central gutter. Above the image, in the top margin, a single line of text also spans the gutter. The composition is largely symmetrical around the vertical axis.

typography.display_font_style: Sans-serif, medium weight, medium size, lowercase. Positioned in the white space at the top of the spread.
special_treatments: The text is a sentence fragment ending in an ellipsis, suggesting continuation.

color_and_tone.description: The stark black-and-white photography creates a dramatic tonal range, from the deep black of the pupil to the bright white of the sclera and surrounding page.

design.left_right_relationship: The two pages are unified into a single canvas, bisected by the gutter. The reader's brain must stitch the two halves of the eye together, actively overcoming the physical separation of the pages.

spread_description: A massive, high-contrast photograph of a human eye stares directly out at the reader, spanning the entire two-page spread and split by the book's spine. Above this confrontational and intimate image, a single line of text reads 'is an extension of the eye...'. The scale and direct gaze create a powerful, slightly unsettling experience of being seen.
